name: Determine and Apply Image Orientation Correction
description: Standard astronomical convention dictates that images be oriented with celestial North pointing up and East pointing left. Real telescope images are almost never taken in this orientation. Your task is to correct the orientation of the Hubble Space Telescope image located at ~/AstroImages/orientation/ngc6652_555w.fits. Determine the current position angle from the WCS keywords in the FITS header (refer to ~/AstroImages/orientation/orientation_hints.txt for guidance) and rotate the image so that North is up and East is left. Save the corrected FITS file to ~/AstroImages/orientation/corrected/ngc6652_555w_northup.fits. Create a text report at ~/AstroImages/orientation/corrected/orientation_report.txt including the WCS keywords found (CD1_1, CD1_2, CD2_1, CD2_2), the computed position angle of North in degrees, and the rotation angle applied.
